name: South Korea
summary: KOSPI AT 5,373.77 — RELIEF RALLY +6.4%: Largest single-session surge in months on triple catalyst (Pezeshkian peace + exports +48.3% beat + bargain hunting). Samsung +6.28%, SK hynix +7.56%. BUT: fabs STILL on 50% helium allocations (confirmed rationing). Won weakened to 1,499 despite equity rally. All three rally legs fragile — exports backward-looking, peace is rhetoric, helium crisis unresolved. +24.7% 3M — how much good news is priced?

price_snapshots.trigger: Largest single-session rally in months — triple catalyst
price_snapshots.delta_context: Surged +6.4% from 5,052 on: (1) Pezeshkian peace signal (helium crisis resolves if war ends), (2) Korean March exports +48.3% YoY (beat +44.9% consensus), (3) bargain hunting after -4.26% prior session. Samsung +6.28%, SK hynix +7.56%. Won weakened to 1,499.

signals.content: KOSPI crashed 6.5% triggering circuit breaker — worst single-day drop since COVID. Samsung Electronics fell 8.2%. SK Hynix fell 9.1%. Driven by oil spike + helium supply concerns + global risk-off. (Sources: yfinance, Bloomberg, Reuters)

KOSPI at 5,438.87 (-0.4% daily, -10.6% monthly). Two semiconductor fabs announced reduced output due to helium allocation restrictions following Ras Laffan damage. USD/KRW at 1,508 — weakest since 2022. South Korea's energy import dependence (~70% from Gulf) makes it acutely exposed to Hormuz disruption. (Sources: yfinance, industry reports)

KOSPI triple cascade confirmed: helium→semiconductor fabs (~40% of -10.6% monthly decline, ~4.2%), Google TurboQuant AI memory compression (~25%, ~2.7%), foreign capital flight (~35%, ~3.7%). Foreigners dumped 51.4 trillion won ($37.8B) since February — largest outflow since Global Financial Crisis. Samsung foreign ownership hit 48.9% — lowest since October 2013 (12.5-year low). Won at 1,508/USD — weakest since March 2009 (17-year low). TrendForce's March 24 'two-week clock' on helium inventories means ~8 days remain before Asian chip fab inventories exhaust at full production rates. The helium cascade path (war→Qatar→helium→semiconductors→KOSPI) validated by 3 independent sources (TrendForce, Nikkei Asia, Korea International Trade Association). (Sources: yfinance, TrendForce, Korea International Trade Association)

KOSPI deepened decline to 5,281.76 (-2.9% Monday, -16.3% monthly). Foreign capital flight accelerated: ₩51.4T ($37.8B) sold since Feb — largest since GFC. Samsung foreign ownership at 48.9% (12.5-year low). Won at ₩1,512 (17-year low). Helium data conflict: Samsung claims 6 months stock but TrendForce says industry at ~6 days. SK hynix likely more vulnerable than Samsung. (Sources: yfinance, Korea International Trade Association, TrendForce, Korea Plus)

KOSPI broke 5,100 support — fell -3.4% to 5,099.97 on March 31 (LIVE data). Worst-performing major index: -18.3% monthly. SK hynix -5.8%, Samsung -3.5%. Won at ₩1,533.72 (17-year low, +1.8% daily). Triple cascade confirmed: helium (~40%), energy dependence (~25%), foreign capital flight (~35%). TrendForce helium clock: ~6-8 days to Asian fab exhaustion. Samsung claims 6-month buffer via HeRS recycling system. No confirmed production halt yet. Next level: 5,000 (psychological).

KOSPI closed at 5,052.46 (-4.26%) — worst performing major index globally in March (-19.1% monthly). Approaching bear market territory. Samsung -5.2%, SK hynix -5.2% — gap narrowed, weakening Samsung HeRS differentiation thesis. Foreign investors sold 35 trillion won in March (all-time monthly record). Won at 17-year low (₩1,524.62). Triple whammy: helium cascade (~30%), Google TurboQuant (~40%), high chip prices (~30%). KOSPI broke below 5,100 helium cascade threshold. Next level: 5,000 (psychological). CONFIRMED.

KOSPI surged +6.4% to 5,373.77 — largest single-session rally in months. Triple catalyst: (1) Pezeshkian peace signal → if war ends, Qatar helium resumes, fab crisis resolves (crisis relief ~2.9%). (2) Korean March exports +48.3% YoY — blowing past consensus +44.9%, semiconductor exports continuing 10+ month expansion streak (fundamentals ~3.5%). (3) Bargain hunting after prior session's -4.26% plunge. Samsung +6.28%, SK hynix +7.56% — both rallying on export data + helium relief. Won weakened to ₩1,499 despite equity rally (foreign selling into strength). Fabs still on 50% helium allocations (CONFIRMED rationing). RELIEF RALLY, not resolution — all three legs fragile. Exports backward-looking, peace is single statement, helium rationing real NOW. +6.4% on top of +24.7% 3M raises: how much good news priced? (Sources: CNBC, Seoul Economic Daily, yfinance, Gasworld)

edges.context: Samsung and SK Hynix depend on helium for semiconductor fabrication. KOSPI surged +6.4% on Pezeshkian peace signal (helium relief). BUT fabs STILL on 50% helium allocations (confirmed rationing). Relief rally, not resolution.
South Korea imports ~70% of energy from the Gulf. Oil above $100 adds massive import costs. Won weakened to 1,499 despite equity rally — foreign selling into strength.
Majority of Korean energy imports transit Hormuz. Closure directly impacts energy security and industrial production.

trigger_points.condition: Samsung or SK Hynix announces production halt due to helium shortage
trigger_points.mechanism: Confirmed fab shutdown would cascade to global semiconductor supply, AI infrastructure, and tech valuations worldwide. Currently only 'reduced output' reported, not full halt.
